description: Architectural decision framework for the AutoAgent meta-agent harness engineering system

fixed_adapter_immutability.description: The FIXED ADAPTER section in agent.py and agent-claude.py must not be modified without explicit human instruction. It contains the Harbor integration boundary, ATIF serialization, and the AutoAgent(BaseAgent) class.
rules: Never modify code below the FIXED ADAPTER BOUNDARY comment
Never modify the AutoAgent class structure, SUPPORTS_ATIF flag, or trajectory serialization
Never change the Harbor adapter's run() method signature or behavior
If the human asks for adapter changes, confirm explicitly before proceeding

single_file_harness.description: Each harness is intentionally a single file. Do not split into modules. Simplicity is a first-class design goal.
rules: Keep all harness code in agent.py (or agent-claude.py) — no helper modules
Do not create separate files for tools, agents, or utilities
If a helper is needed, add it as an inner function or module-level function in the same file

no_overfitting.description: Harness improvements must be general, not task-specific. Benchmark-specific keyword rules and hardcoded solutions are forbidden.
rules: Apply the overfitting test: 'If this exact task disappeared, would this still be a worthwhile improvement?'
Do not add task-specific conditions, keyword matching, or hardcoded solutions
Do not add benchmark-specific heuristics that only help on known task types
Prefer fixing a class of failures, not a single failing task

simplicity_wins.description: All else being equal, simpler is better. If a change achieves the same 'passed' result with a simpler harness, keep it. Complexity that does not improve scores must be removed.
rules: Do not add components, logic, or prompts that do not improve pass rate
When two approaches achieve the same score, always choose the simpler one
Remove special-case handling that does not demonstrably help
Prefer fewer tools over more tools when the simpler set performs equally

docker_isolation.description: All agent execution happens inside Docker containers. Never run agent code directly on the host.
rules: Always use 'uv run harbor run ...' to invoke the agent
Never execute agent.py or agent-claude.py directly on the host for task runs
Always rebuild the base image after changing agent.py or pyproject.toml
Clean jobs/ before each new run to avoid stale results

metric_driven_decisions.description: Keep/discard decisions must be based on the 'passed' metric. Subjective improvements without metric evidence must not be kept.
rules: Keep if 'passed' improved
Keep if 'passed' is equal and the harness is simpler
Discard everything else, even if the change seems reasonable
Always run the full benchmark before deciding to keep or discard
Record every run in results.tsv regardless of outcome

secrets_management.description: API keys and credentials must never be committed to the repository.
rules: Never commit .env or any file containing API keys
Never hardcode API keys in agent.py, Dockerfile.base, or pyproject.toml
Use environment variables or .env (gitignored) for all credentials
Verify .gitignore includes .env before any commit touching configuration

model_constraint.description: The model must not be changed from the default (gpt-5 in agent.py, haiku in agent-claude.py) without explicit human instruction.
rules: Do not change MODEL without explicit human permission
If testing a different model, confirm with the human first
Document model changes in results.tsv with the reason

new_tool: Identify the class of failures the tool addresses
Verify the tool is general (not task-specific)
Add the tool to create_tools() in the editable section
Keep if passed improved or harness is simpler at same score
prompt_change: Identify which failure pattern the prompt change addresses
Draft the change to be general, not task-specific
Keep or discard per metric rules
dependency_addition: Verify the package is needed for a general tool capability
Add to Dockerfile.base if the package needs system-level setup
Verify agent imports cleanly: uv run python -c 'from agent import AutoAgent'
Run baseline before testing the improvement
harness_refactor: Confirm the refactor does not touch the FIXED ADAPTER section
Confirm the refactor preserves single-file layout
Rebuild and run a sanity check import
Keep only if simpler at same score or better
